newsletter.pageMetaDescription: Subscribe to TheMusicTree email updates. Double opt-in: confirm from your inbox to join the list.
newsletter.pageIntro: Occasional updates on projects and the open music-data ecosystem. After you subscribe, we send a confirmation email — you are only added once you confirm (double opt-in).
newsletter.pageContactEnd: for other ways to reach the team.
newsletter.confirmedMetaDescription: Your email subscription to TheMusicTree updates is active.
newsletter.confirmedIntro: Thanks — you are on the list. You can close this tab or continue browsing the site.
newsletter.contributeSectionLead: Occasional updates on releases, roadmap, and community news.

newsletterForm.success: Check your inbox to confirm your subscription.

project.documentationIntro: Setup, APIs, architecture, and contribution workflows live in each repository's README (and linked resources). This page focuses on the product story and ecosystem fit.

teaserSummaries.audiometa-python: Unified Python library for reading and writing audio metadata (ID3v1, ID3v2, Vorbis, RIFF). Live web demo available.
teaserSummaries.audiometa-webapp: Web app for reading and editing audio metadata (ID3, Vorbis, RIFF) in the browser. Companion to AudioMeta Python.
teaserSummaries.grow-the-music-tree: Interactive map of global music genres. Community-driven genre tree, personalized music journeys, and intelligent genre detection. API access via TheMusicTreeAPI.
teaserSummaries.the-music-tree-api: RESTful API for genre hierarchy and metadata, intelligent genre detection and classification, personalized user profiling, and data-driven recommendations. Built with Django REST Framework and PostgreSQL.
teaserSummaries.hear-the-music-tree: Cloud-based audio file manager for collectors, DJs, and music lovers. Smart playlists, universal format and tag support, secure cloud storage, cross-platform sync and export. Powered by GrowTheMusicTree.

contribute.title: Contribute
contribute.intro: We love collaborating with the community. Use the channels below to get started.

whereToStart.intro: Choose where you want to begin — each option opens the right place to chat, browse code, report bugs, or discuss ideas.
whereToStart.discordDescription: Chat with contributors, ask quick questions, and follow project updates in real time.
whereToStart.githubDescription: Explore repositories, read code and READMEs, and fork a project when you are ready to propose changes.
whereToStart.issuesDescription: Find reported bugs and planned work across org repositories, or open a new issue in the repo that best fits your report.
whereToStart.discussionsDescription: Share ideas, ask broader questions, and weigh trade-offs before turning something into a formal issue or pull request.

contact.title: Connect With Us
contact.channelsIntro: Pick a channel — each link opens the right place for issues, discussions, chat, or social updates.
contact.issuesDescription: Find reported bugs and planned work across org repositories, or open a new issue in the repo that best fits your report.
contact.discussionsDescription: Share ideas, ask broader questions, and weigh trade-offs before turning something into a formal issue or pull request.
contact.discordDescription: Chat with contributors, ask quick questions, and follow project updates in real time.
contact.socialDescription: Follow the project or send a direct message on these channels.
contact.openToText: Collaborations and connecting with fellow developers and music technology enthusiasts.
